stepAnswer: The limit is $$4$$, but f(0) does not exist
stepTitle: Consider $$f(x)=x^2+4$$ for $$x\ne$$ $$0$$, $$with$$ $$no$$ $$value$$ $$defined$$ $$at$$ $$x=0$$. $$If$$ $$you$$ $$graph$$ $$it$$, $$it$$ $$looks$$ $$like$$ $$the$$ $$parabola$$ $$y=x^2+4$$ with a hole at $$(0,4)$$. $$Which$$ $$statement$$ $$is$$ $$true$$?
answerLatex: The limit is $$4$$, but f(0) does not exist
choices: The limit is $$4$$, but f(0) does not exist
The limit does not exist because f(0) is undefined
